description_long: This government building features a grand, rectangular stone structure with a prominent central cupola topped by a weather vane and cross. The roof is made of aged, patinated metal panels, and the facade is adorned with classical columns and a pediment above the main entrance. The rear of the building is distinguished by a large, semi-circular portico supported by tall columns, creating an imposing and formal appearance. Multiple rows of large, evenly spaced windows line the sides, providing symmetry and rhythm to the exterior. The building sits on a raised foundation with a series of steps leading up to the main entrance, emphasizing its civic importance. The overall design is stately and monumental, reflecting traditional government architecture.
description: A stately stone government building with a central cupola, classical columns, and a semi-circular rear portico. The structure features a patinated metal roof and grand entrance steps.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the main entrance with a columned portico, pediment, and the central cupola rising above the roof.
description_view_1: The left side view shows the building's long facade with rows of windows and the prominent cupola, as well as the curved rear portico.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the large semi-circular portico supported by columns and the symmetrical arrangement of the roof and cupola.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, displaying the extended facade, window rows, and the rounded rear portico.
attribute_public_entrance_design: The main public entrance features a grand staircase leading up to a portico supported by classical columns and topped with a decorative pediment, providing a formal and accessible entryway.
